item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked this a lot, evoked some strong emotional reactions

Slight nitpick:

Unknowingly recruted alongside its host

*recruited

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The concept itself is interesting. However, even in the document proper, it is somewhat unclear what exactly is going on. There is confusion between the subject and the SCP in multiple instances, and the "feeding" part is not elaborated on at all, among other issues.

The stuff between the SCP itself has no reason to be here in-universe. Why would the Foundation store this SCP like this in their database? Format Screws shouldn't be used willy-nilly like this without any justification.

I'm giving this one a downvote until it's edited to a higher standard. It has potential, but as it stands it just really needs to be rewritten.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have a few problems with this. While I like the way this was told, it feels fairly similar to the timelords from Doctor Who, or at least the Doctor. Despite this, if that was an intentional inclusion, this was done correctly.

There is also an issue in how the O5 council reacts. They don't typically allow anomalies to go uncontained like this, even with a suggestion from a Site Director. I will admit there are exceptions, such as SCP-999, yet these are considered safe in that they don't typically spread to other personnel. On top of this, they are lethal to the host if they end up dying.

Finally, you state at one point that SCP-3623 cannot die, yet later on state they can. I may be misinterpreting this, but it feels somewhat confusing.

Despite this, I still found the story the article was attached to compelling and somewhat saddening. No vote for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You can tell that this article definitely has had lots of hard work put into it. My only complaints are similar to the ones above. First: The phrasing is a bit awkward, but with some edits it should be fine. Second: The hosts should be named SCP-3623-1 in my opinion because at times I was slightly confused with who you were talking about. But other then that, the article kept my interest and was extremely creative. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you. I don't think I would ever notice those.
I have a gift which allows me to read anytihng without breaking focus even if there are mistakes. In a case like this however, when I need to see the mistakes, it is a curse.

Will get onto it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm just gonna list a few while reading from the start, so that is by no mean supposed to be exhaustive nor necesserally the most important examples. Also I don't pretend that suggested replacements are the best possibilities, just what I personally would change it for.

examples
– hide block

the means of this are however unknown

The "this" feel inappropriately placed or redundant. I think "by unknown means" would flow better

they are not in any immediate danger of life.

Same here, the qualitative seems misplaced. Maybe "immediate lethal danger" or just "immediate danger" would be better.

only when host

"only when the host"

A few cases of it's <-> its too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, he cannot set her free, she will just get a new host, the point was that she will be a new person by than and the problem gets solved, she will disappear from the records and by the time they will find her, she will probably be considered as a different instance. Problem solved. I guess. I am planning on making a tale focusing on this.

He could have helped her out of the Foundation but that would require a difficult plan and there was no time for that. Put it on my laziness or unwillingness to complicate it more.

You think so? I would say that it is the perfect spot for it, putting it somewhere else would just make it awkard somewhere else.

Anyaway. Thanks for reading and upvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you. I am very happy that you see it this way.

Interesting, could you give me an example of what you mean?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i think my main complaint is i dont think i understand the doctors plan? if he wanted his muse to be free, shouldnt he have messaged her telling her to get out or something? and i really dont think the foundation would be fooled by him saying he destroyed the body. ultimately theyd just still find a woman with amnesia and figure out who she was wouldnt they?

i do like the idea that him dying could allow her to escape because he could disguise it as a consequence of her death, but i just think there needs to be more of a plan beyond just that

other than that, i feel like some of the content isnt really in ideal order, like for example im not sure the first paragraph of the containment procedures should be there, and probably shouldn't be in the containment procedures at all? it sounds more like part of the description

+1 though, i like the story well enough and i think itd be decent with some tweaks

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I very much like the concept of this, and especially the fact that it is a completely innocuous but still anomalous phenomenon, making this a great example of why an SCP doesn't have to kill you to be interesting.

Now on a less positive side I do think there is a bit of an issue with awkward phrasing and maybe a bit of grammar, that really impact the read of the article negatively since you kind of "bump" on the wording sometimes.
Still an upvote for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: very interesting. just had a problem with the difference between the hosts and the skip. Kind of got confusing at the end.other than that, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you. More help with grammatical errors is always welcome (Especially since some people actually give you the opposite of "useful fix" and make it even worse.)

As for the SCP-3623-1 (or something along those lines), I as trying to stay away from that since last time I tried, people told me they do not understand what I as going for. But I will take a look at it.

I am glad you enjoyed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the idea behind this, but the article could use some work. First off, there are still a few grammatical errors ("personnel is" should be "personnel are", for example) and bits of awkward wording ("however they are not in any immediate danger of life"). Also, you may want to make the separation between host and symbiont more clear - perhaps by calling the hosts SCP-3623-1 or something along those lines.

That being said, I enjoyed the story at the core of the article, and you have structured it in an interesting way. +1.

item.title: Thanks.
item.content:encoded:
I finally made myself publish this, the idea has been in my head for a few months now and this text existed for almost as long. Anyway, too many people helped me with critique and mostly grammar checks so I can't possibly name all of them but know that without you, this wouldn't be possible.

Thank you.
